title: REGRESSION: preemptive_generation=true killed ALL background tool calls (no attribution, no notes)
description: Live call 2026-07-17 21:48 PT (session f1ebc3ba): 5+ min of conversation, zero submit_background_query events, zero MCP POSTs at the backend — no attribution ping, no notebook, so a call-back would remember nothing. Config diff vs last working call: preemptive_generation flipped true earlier today (plus eot 0.7 / tts turbo / history 60 / ambient — none plausibly affect tool use). VB's own setting description warns preemptive gen 'may produce less accurate responses'. Reverted to false at 21:57 PT; new sessions should tool-call again. Verify on the next call, then bd close. If confirmed, never re-enable on cascaded_concierge.
close_reason: CONFIRMED live: zero tool calls while preemptive_generation=true, resumed at 21:57:19 the moment it flipped false (same session). Reverted permanently; memory updated. Never enable on cascaded_concierge.

title: Migrate live labs user data: gems.db profiles/notes -> account dossiers, drop tables
description: One-off migration on labs: profiles+notes rows in data/gems.db move to ~/data/Projects/vox-local/accounts{,-pending}/<n>/ (persona.md/trip.md via topic routing); legacy notes.txt lines re-routed; then DROP TABLE notes,profiles + VACUUM and commit the shrunken gems.db. Must run BEFORE deploying code that drops tables on connect.
close_reason: Closed

title: Import Meridian and migrate it to xAI realtime voice
description: Preserve Vincent's standalone Meridian branch inside this repo under meridian/, then replace Meridian's browser Web Speech + Claude SSE conversation stack with xAI's browser-safe realtime Voice Agent API while retaining itinerary, camera, tool-feed, text-input, and keyless-demo behavior.
design: Use a subtree-style unrelated-history merge: ours merge parent plus read-tree --prefix=meridian/. For xAI, mint ephemeral secrets server-side, connect from the browser via xai-client-secret subprotocol, stream PCM16 audio, execute existing UI mutations as xAI custom function calls, and keep built-in web_search server-side.
acceptance_criteria: 1. origin/meridian is integrated into main as a merge parent and its app tree lives under meridian/ without overwriting vox-local root files. 2. Browser clients obtain only short-lived xAI client secrets from a server route; XAI_API_KEY never enters client bundles. 3. Voice input and output use xAI Realtime Voice Agent at 24 kHz PCM with interruption and server VAD; Web Speech and Anthropic are removed. 4. xAI custom tools continue to mutate itinerary, camera, chips, and visible tool feed; text input uses the same realtime session. 5. Missing key/network/mic failures degrade visibly and the honest local mock remains usable for typed demos. 6. Meridian type-check/build and the existing Python regression suite pass.
notes: BUILD: imported origin/meridian under meridian/ with preserved merge parent; replaced Web Speech + Claude SSE with xAI realtime client-secret/WebSocket flow; added direct contributor commands/docs. VERIFY so far: real xAI token 200, realtime WebSocket, itinerary custom tool, streamed reply, and fake-microphone browser flow passed; 64 Python tests passed. Follow-up voice-local-9gp tracks the pre-existing moderate Next/PostCSS audit advisory.
close_reason: Imported origin/meridian under meridian/ with the original commit preserved as a merge parent. Replaced Web Speech and Claude SSE with xAI realtime voice using server-minted client secrets, 24 kHz PCM, server VAD, transcription, streamed playback, web search, and client-side itinerary/camera/suggestion tools. Added Vincent's direct contributor workflow, CODEOWNERS, root make shortcuts, and a validation profile. Verified xAI token HTTP 200, realtime WebSocket, rendered one-day Tokyo itinerary, streamed reply, fake-microphone listen/stop, zero browser errors, clean typecheck/build/security scan, and all 64 Python tests.

title: Support long-form outbound call briefs
description: Expand the bulk outbound call API from a short target to a detailed operator brief with long-form request, optional do/don't guidance, and optional agent-fit context; preserve target compatibility and relay the complete brief to Puffo/Koyuki.
design: Normalize target/description into one rendered operator brief. Keep a short thread title/preview but post the full body in the root thread before dialing. Use a 12k character cap, optional bounded string lists for dos/donts, and optional agent_fit text. The same full rendered brief becomes the shared batch outbound goal.
acceptance_criteria:
- The outbound endpoint accepts a long-form brief up to 12,000 characters rather than restricting the user request to 2,000 characters.
- Optional do/don't lists and agent-fit context are validated, rendered legibly in the per-call Puffo thread, and delivered to Koyuki as outbound context.
- Existing callers using target continue to work; malformed/empty briefs are rejected with no calls created.
- Tests cover the expanded brief and compatibility behavior.
close_reason: Expanded bulk outbound calls to accept a 12,000-character description plus optional dos, donts, and agent_fit. The complete rendered brief is posted in every per-call Puffo thread and delivered to Koyuki; legacy target remains accepted. Verified 64 tests and restarted the live service.

title: Add bulk outbound call thread relay API
description: Add a vox-local API that accepts up to five phone numbers and a target objective, starts outbound Koyuki calls concurrently, creates one Puffo thread per call in the configured target channel, and mirrors completed user/agent transcript turns into the corresponding thread.
design: Introduce an OutboundCallRelay service with a Vocal Bridge REST client and a singleton debug-event poller. Match VB sessions by the room name returned from POST /api/v1/calls; relay only user_transcription and agent_response events into per-call Puffo thread roots. Use PUFFO_CHANNEL_ID and VOICE_LOCAL_OUTBOUND_TOKEN, store only in-memory run mapping for MVP, mask recipient labels in Puffo, and require consent_to_call to avoid an unauthenticated robocall surface.
acceptance_criteria:
- POST /api/outbound/calls accepts 1–5 unique E.164 phone numbers, a non-empty target description, and explicit consent, behind a dedicated bearer token.
- The route starts outbound calls concurrently and returns a run/call record without exposing full phone numbers.
- Each call gets a separate Puffo thread in the configured target channel before dialing; status and every finished VB user/agent transcript event are appended to the matching thread with clear [User]/[Agent] prefixes.
- Requests reject invalid input, missing consent, missing outbound configuration, and invalid auth without creating calls.
- Tests cover validation, concurrent dispatch, per-call thread creation, event-to-thread routing, and no duplicate transcript post.
notes: No real numbers will be dialed during verification. Vocal Bridge debug mode is already enabled and its /api/v1/debug/events endpoint carries session-scoped transcript events.
close_reason: Implemented and activated the authenticated bulk outbound relay: 1–5 consented E.164 recipients, parallel VB dialing, dedicated configured-channel Puffo thread per call, final [User]/[Agent] debug-transcript mirroring with dedupe, target delivery on first check_updates, and a live endpoint validation probe that placed no calls. Verified 63 tests plus wheel build.

title: Enable Koyuki outbound calling
description: Enable the Vocal Bridge outbound-calling capability for Koyuki, record the live configuration change in the required VB⇄vox-local flow document, and verify the setting.
design: Use vb config set --outbound-enabled true --accept-outbound-tos. Update only the capability/topology facts and changelog in the project-vault flow document; no prompt or backend behavior changes are needed.
acceptance_criteria: - Vocal Bridge confirms outbound calling is enabled and its terms are accepted for Koyuki.\n- The VB⇄vox-local flow document records that Koyuki can place outbound calls and its changelog includes the configuration change.\n- The repository tracking record is closed, committed, and pushed.
close_reason: Enabled Koyuki outbound calling with Vocal Bridge terms accepted; CLI confirmed updated outbound_enabled and outbound_tos_accepted. Updated the required project-vault flow.md capability/changelog record.

title: MCP as a running system: in-memory write queue + worker; VB post-call callback reconciles
description: Boyan: VB should send multiple requests; vox-local keeps running with a worker processing them; all VB write requests enter an in-memory queue first and docs keep updating as it goes; VB triggers a post-phone-call callback into this MCP so state ends in good shape. Design: write ops (remember, caller_name) enqueue + instant SILENT ack, drained by a persistent worker under the store lock; read/gated ops stay synchronous. New post_call_notes op attaches by caller_phone (fallback: last closed call), trusted to write directly to matched accounts; VB post-processing configured with our MCP URL (?key auth) + archivist prompt.
close_reason: Shipped: intake queue + worker live (59 tests), post_call_notes op deployed, VB post-processing configured (archivist prompt + MCP URL). Verify on next real call's post-processing.

title: Build creator portal MVP
description: Create a polished local web portal for creators to add and manage guide gems, see their content, and review recommendation counts/context. Visual direction should draw from puffo-style.png.
design: Add a lightweight, dependency-free HTML/CSS/JS portal served from the existing FastAPI app. Extend SQLite schema with recommendation events and expose creator-facing JSON endpoints plus static portal. Instrument the existing get_gem service path to record served context using only non-PII metadata.
acceptance_criteria:
- A local creator portal is served by vox-local with a polished Puffo-inspired visual system.
- Creators can create a complete gem through a validated form and immediately see it among their content.
- The portal lists database gems with usable search/filtering and summary counts.
- Each gem exposes recommendation count plus recent served-context records; tracking is recorded whenever the voice backend returns a gem.
- Automated tests cover the portal API and recommendation tracking without breaking existing endpoints.
notes: MVP assumes one local creator identity; no auth or multi-creator attribution yet. Preserve current /api/gems consumer contract.
close_reason: Delivered a local /creator Field Notes portal with creation, collection/search, aggregate recommendation counts, recent privacy-safe served context, and voice get_gem instrumentation. Verified with uv run python -m pytest tests -q (56 passed).

title: VB PLATFORM BUG: foreground sessions intermittently launch with no working tool access (3 of 5 tonight)
description: Sessions 6cb8fc6f (48 msgs), 2da3c4a0, and f1ebc3ba's first 9 min made zero submit_background_query calls; model roleplays 'I checked, nothing on file'. Identical prompt/config sessions minutes apart tool-call fine (f3797061). Suspect a session-startup race (6cb8fc6f started 13s before the previous session finalized). Mitigations shipped: imperative first-ping, spoken-check-must-be-real, admit-the-hiccup rules. ACTION: Boyan to report to VB with these session IDs; retest after their fix.
notes: ESCALATED EVIDENCE 2026-07-18: tool-skipping confirmed at BOTH layers. (1) Foreground: sessions 830ba672 (07-18 08:55), 6cb8fc6f, 2da3c4a0 — zero submit_background_query entire call. (2) Background: session b7e746db (07-18 08:56) — jobs at 08:59:11/08:59:30/08:59:52/09:00:14 PT each did MCP initialize + ListTools against our server and NEVER sent CallToolRequest, then reported '[OK] Filed.' to the foreground — fabricated success; caller's notes (one month, mid-September, solo) permanently lost. Working queries in the same call went initialize->CallToolRequest directly. Our ListTools verifiably returns query_backend. Report to VB with these session IDs + timestamps.

Shipped caller_name op (52 tests), prompt mandates ping-first and asks fly-from/fly-back when relevant. Also documented VB dead-session-on-quick-redial (6cb8fc6f, 0 events, stuck in_progress).
close_reason: Shipped: backend restarted with caller_name, prompt 20563 chars live, flow.md updated, 626084 name set to Boyan Lin manually.

Pending profile parked within first seconds guarantees call-backs are remembered. Shipped + live.

title: Call opening: greeting asks the name; prompt settles it in the first exchange
description: Boyan: when no account/name is known, the immediate next step after pickup is getting the name. The live VB greeting ('How are you doing today?') had drifted from the prompt's assumed line — set greeting to 'Hello, こんにちは. My name is Koyuki. Who do I have the pleasure to speak with?' and rewrote YOUR GREETING: known caller -> skip the question, name given -> use it, no name in first words -> answer briefly and fold the ask into the same turn, drop it gracefully if declined.
close_reason: Greeting set on VB + YOUR GREETING rewritten with attribution-aware name-ask variants; prompt pushed live (18597 chars)

title: Verify + harden call-start attribution: known account greeted by name (PIN for details), pending account info free
description: Boyan: at call start VB must relay caller info to vox-local; known account -> name immediately, details after PIN; pending account -> use last-call info freely. Mechanism exists (services.attribute + first-ping check_updates + constructor caller-ID match). Verify E2E with a synthetic pending account through the public MCP, add unit coverage for the two preload briefs, and strengthen prompt if the first ping isn't reliably first.
close_reason: Verified live (pending notebook preloads on first ping for +6506567722) + fixed the silent no-name gap for known accounts with empty dossiers; regression tests added. NOTE: accounts store is empty since sunset-2026-07-15 — name-greeting for real accounts activates once accounts exist again.

title: Latency round 2: eot 0.7, faster TTS, trimmed history + office ambience instead of keyboard
description: Boyan: thinking time still slow; wants background noise that is not keyboard (street/cafe). VB only offers 'subtle office ambience' (ambient_enabled) — enable at low volume. Latency: eot_threshold 0.8->0.7, tts eleven_multilingual_v2 -> eleven_turbo_v2_5 (code-switch quality vs flash), max_history 100->60 to stop long-call TTFT creep. All via model-settings merge; backup already in repo.
close_reason: Applied live: eot_threshold 0.7, tts eleven_turbo_v2_5, max_history 60, ambient office noise ON @0.3 (VB has no street/cafe option; keyboard 'thinking' stays off). Rollback: vb config set --model-settings-file data/model-settings-backup-2026-07-17.json

title: Prompt: planning mode — draft-first itinerary co-planning, not serial interviewing (tester feedback #2)
description: Tester feedback: planning a trip by phone is much slower than text chat; agent asks detail questions one at a time. The persona is deliberately explore-first, but once the caller shifts to planning she should propose a concrete skeleton (cities/nights/anchors) in one breath and iterate on reactions; max one question per turn, bundle small details. Add a WHEN THEY WANT A PLAN block to docs-agent-prompt.txt.
close_reason: Closed

title: Prompt: instant spoken ack before backend-gated answers (tester feedback #1)
description: Tester feedback: multi-second dead air before replies feels robotic since typing sounds were removed. Add a NEVER-LEAVE-DEAD-AIR rule to docs-agent-prompt.txt: when a reply depends on a backend query (verify, booking ops, lookups), speak one short varied natural beat in the same turn before/while the query runs. Push via vb prompt set --file and commit.
close_reason: Closed

title: VB config: enable preemptive_generation (latency floor cut on every turn)
description: Per-turn gap is 3.5-6s median even with no tools; preemptive_generation=false means the foreground only starts generating after the 0.8 EOT decision. Turn it on so generation overlaps the end-of-turn wait. Backup model-settings JSON first; rollback = vb config set --model-settings-file backup.
close_reason: Closed

title: Ops: ngrok tunnel died silently Jul 16 — Restart=always + public healthz monitoring
description: voice-local-ngrok.service exited cleanly ('received stop request', likely another ngrok grabbing the free-plan session) and stayed dead; Koyuki took the Jul 17 tester call with zero backend tools and nothing alerted. Fix now: Restart=always + RestartSec in the unit. Follow-up: LabOps-style check that curls https://lula-tasteless-businesslike.ngrok-free.dev/healthz and alerts Discord when it fails.
notes: DONE: Restart=always + RestartSec=15 applied to voice-local-ngrok.service, daemon-reloaded, tunnel verified 200 through public URL. REMAINING: external healthz monitor (curl public /healthz -> Discord alert) so a dead tunnel never again goes unnoticed until a tester calls.
close_reason: Restart=always on ngrok unit + watchdog timer (healthz -> self-heal -> Discord alert) enabled and verified

title: Kill the ~2.5s/query resolve tax: stale-while-revalidate session cache in VBBackend
description: Every query_backend call after the 2s RESOLVE_CACHE_S pays a blocking ~2.5s VB logs-API round trip inside _resolve_call (and up to 14.7s of retries on a miss) while holding the query lock. Measured 2026-07-17: the actual query executed in 9ms. Fix: serve the cached (sid, phone) immediately once known and refresh it in the background; block only when no resolution exists yet (cold start). Invalidate the cache when a call closes so the next call re-resolves fresh.
close_reason: Closed

title: Latency: pin VB background model to claude-haiku-4-5 + bridge-beat prompt tweaks
description: Measured: background inference dominates MCP latency (3-4s per leg, ListTools->CallTool gaps in journal); tunnel is 0.22s/RTT. Change: model-settings merge background.model=claude-haiku-4-5 (fresh backup data/model-settings-backup-2026-07-18.json committed first); prompt NEVER-LEAVE-DEAD-AIR upgraded with (a) bridge-beat: beat + one substantive related sentence for long async waits, (b) second-beat permission when a result hasn't landed by next turn. Verification: on Boyan's next call, grep journal — every BG '[OK] Filed.' needs a matching 'query_backend <-'; compare fabrication rate vs sonnet-4-6 baseline (07-18 08:59 case); flip back if worse.
notes: Shipped 2026-07-18 ~09:35 PT: background.model=claude-haiku-4-5 (merge-set, verified; preemptive_generation still false), prompt bridge-beat + second-beat pushed live (21851 chars), flow.md updated (topology/latency/changelog). PENDING VERIFICATION: fabrication A/B on Boyan's next call — grep journal, every BG '[OK] Filed.' needs matching 'query_backend <-'; flip back via data/model-settings-backup-2026-07-18.json if worse than sonnet-4-6 baseline.

title: Post-call notebook rewrite via VB post-processing -> MCP (the deeper async-notes lever)
description: In-call notes are now batched fire-and-forget, but the deepest version of 'notes as side-work' is moving comprehensive note-taking OUT of the call: VB post-processing runs after each call with the full transcript and can call our MCP (post-processing-mcp-url supports the ?key= auth we already use). Design: a post_call_notes op that attaches to the most recently closed call state (post-call there is no in_progress session to resolve), rewrites running summaries from the whole transcript, and dedupes against in-call notes. Then the prompt can trim in-call noting to only what the call itself needs. Needs: op + last-closed-caller tracking + vb config set --post-processing-prompt/--post-processing-mcp-url + test via 'vb post-processing test'.

title: Rename repo and checkout to vox-local
description: User requested renaming the current voice-local project to vox-local, including the git repo identity, local checkout path, and repo-facing naming/config surfaces that depend on the repo name.
design: Keep the internal Python package directory `src/voice_local` unchanged unless the rename requires import-path churn; update distribution/CLI/service/display names and checkout-path references instead.
acceptance_criteria:
- GitHub repository `lin-labs/voice-local` is renamed to `lin-labs/vox-local` and the local `origin` remote points to the new URL.
- The local checkout directory is renamed from `voice-local` to `vox-local` without losing git history.
- Repo-facing names and paths that depend on the old repo name are updated consistently to `vox-local` where needed, while internal Python module imports remain working.
- Test coverage relevant to the rename passes, and docs/deploy paths no longer point at the old checkout name.
notes: Need to account for Beads bootstrap commit that was created during this session and ensure subsequent commands use the renamed checkout path.
close_reason: Renamed GitHub repo to lin-labs/vox-local, moved the local checkout to /home/blin/Experiments/voice/vox-local, updated repo-facing CLI/service/docs/extension naming, refreshed the venv after the path move, and verified with uv run pytest tests -q plus uv run python -m pytest tests -q.

title: Upgrade Meridian past bundled PostCSS advisory
description: npm audit reports two moderate vulnerabilities because the current Next.js line bundles postcss <8.5.10. The offered npm audit --force resolution is breaking and proposes an invalid downgrade path, so upgrade Next/PostCSS deliberately after confirming a compatible stable release.
acceptance_criteria: npm --prefix meridian audit --omit=dev exits zero; make meridian-check and the Chromium Meridian flow still pass after the dependency update.
